title: Arc2D Object
description: Transient 2D arc. A transient arc is not displayed or saved in a document. Transient arcs are used as a wrapper to work with raw 2D arc information. They are created statically using one of the create methods supported by the Arc2D class.

methods.name: classType
methods.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().

methods.name: copy
methods.description: Creates and returns an independent copy of this Arc2D object.

methods.name: createByCenter
methods.description: Creates a transient 2D arc object specifying the center, radius and start and end angles. A transient arc is not displayed or saved in a document. Transient arcs are used as a wrapper to work with raw 2D arc information.

methods.name: createByThreePoints
methods.description: Creates a transient 2D arc by specifying 3 points. A transient arc is not displayed or saved in a document. Transient arcs are used as a wrapper to work with raw 2D arc information.

methods.name: getData
methods.description: Gets all of the data defining the arc.

methods.name: set
methods.description: Sets all of the data defining the arc.

properties.name: asNurbsCurve
properties.description: Returns a NURBS curve that is geometrically identical to the arc.

properties.name: center
properties.description: Gets and sets the center position of the arc.

properties.name: curveType
properties.description: Returns the type of geometry this curve represents.

properties.name: endAngle
properties.description: Gets and sets the end angle of the arc in radians, where 0 is along the x axis.

properties.name: endPoint
properties.description: Gets the position of the end point of the arc.

properties.name: evaluator
properties.description: Returns an evaluator object that lets you perform additional evaluations on the curve.

properties.name: isClockwise
properties.description: Gets if the sweep direction of the arc is clockwise or counterclockwise.

properties.name: isValid
properties.description: Indicates if this object is still valid, i.e. hasn't been deleted or some other action done to invalidate the reference.

properties.name: objectType
properties.description: This property is supported by all objects in the API and returns a string that contains the full name (namespace::objecttype) describing the type of the object.   It's often useful to use this in combination with the classType method to see if an object is a certain type. For example: if obj.objectType == adsk.core.Point3D.classType():

properties.name: radius
properties.description: Gets and sets the radius of the arc.

properties.name: startAngle
properties.description: Gets and sets the start angle of the arc in radians, where 0 is along the x axis.

properties.name: startPoint
properties.description: Gets the position of the start point of the arc.

asNurbsCurve.title: Arc2D.asNurbsCurve Property
asNurbsCurve.description: Returns a NURBS curve that is geometrically identical to the arc.

syntax.python:
# Get the value of the property.
propertyValue = arc2D_var.asNurbsCurve

classType.title: Arc2D.classType Method
classType.description: Static function that all classes support that returns the type of the class as a string. The returned string matches the string returned by the objectType property. For example if you have a reference to an object and you want to check if it's a SketchLine you can use myObject.objectType == fusion.SketchLine.classType().
classType.return_description: Returns a string indicating the type of the object.

copy.title: Arc2D.copy Method
copy.description: Creates and returns an independent copy of this Arc2D object.
copy.return_description: Returns a new Arc2D object that is a copy of this Arc2D object.

createByCenter.title: Arc2D.createByCenter Method
createByCenter.description: Creates a transient 2D arc object specifying the center, radius and start and end angles. A transient arc is not displayed or saved in a document. Transient arcs are used as a wrapper to work with raw 2D arc information.

syntax.python:
# Uses no optional arguments.
returnValue = adsk.core.Arc2D.createByCenter(center, radius, startAngle, endAngle)

# Uses optional arguments.
returnValue = adsk.core.Arc2D.createByCenter(center, radius, startAngle, endAngle, isClockwise)

createByCenter.return_description: Returns the newly created arc or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: center
parameters.description: A Point2D object that defines the center position of the arc in 2D space.

parameters.name: startAngle
parameters.description: The start angle in radians, where 0 is along the X-axis.

parameters.name: endAngle
parameters.description: The end angle in radians, where 0 is along the X-axis.

parameters.name: isClockwise
parameters.description: Specifies if the sweep of the arc is clockwise or counterclockwise from the start to end angle.   This is an optional argument whose default value is False.

createByThreePoints.title: Arc2D.createByThreePoints Method
createByThreePoints.description: Creates a transient 2D arc by specifying 3 points. A transient arc is not displayed or saved in a document. Transient arcs are used as a wrapper to work with raw 2D arc information.
createByThreePoints.return_description: Returns the newly created arc or null if the creation failed.

parameters.name: startPoint
parameters.description: The start point of the arc.

parameters.name: endPoint
parameters.description: The end point of the arc.

getData.title: Arc2D.getData Method
getData.description: Gets all of the data defining the arc.

syntax.python: (returnValue, center, radius, startAngle, endAngle, isClockwise) = arc2D_var.getData()

parameters.name: center
parameters.description: The output center point of the arc.

parameters.name: radius
parameters.description: The output radius of the arc.

parameters.name: startAngle
parameters.description: The output start angle of the arc in radians, where 0 is along the x axis.

parameters.name: endAngle
parameters.description: The output end angle of the arc in radians, where 0 is along the x axis.

parameters.name: isClockwise
parameters.description: The output value that indicates if the sweep direction is clockwise or counterclockwise.

set.title: Arc2D.set Method
set.description: Sets all of the data defining the arc.

syntax.python: returnValue = arc2D_var.set(center, radius, startAngle, endAngle, isClockwise)
syntax.cpp:
#include 

returnValue = arc2D_var->set(center, radius, startAngle, endAngle, isClockwise);

set.return_description: Returns true if redefining the arc is successful

parameters.name: center
parameters.description: A Point2D object defining the center position of the arc.

parameters.name: startAngle
parameters.description: The start angle of the arc in radians, where 0 is along the x axis.

parameters.name: endAngle
parameters.description: The end angle of the arc in radians, where 0 is along the x axis.

parameters.name: isClockwise
parameters.description: Indicates if the sweep direction is clockwise or counterclockwise.

transformBy.title: Arc2D.transformBy Method
transformBy.return_description: Return true if the transform was successful.

parameters.name: matrix
parameters.description: A 2D matrix that defines the transform to apply to the curve.
